name: Spanish
modelReview: blind 3-persona Sonnet panel + round-trip over the expanded core set (257 patterns: the cell sweep plus the curated spanning set, 2026-06-24). Round-trip 205/205 verified (52 Quartz skipped) — meaning preserved, and the date-or-weekday descriptions are recoverable (a blind agent reconstructs both arms). A full-core-set panel found the date-or-weekday OR-union was the dominant naturalness defect (43 of 63 below-bar items); it was overhauled to a single unified frame — '[month-once] [time], ya sea <DOM-arm> o <DOW-arm>' — fronting the month once (killing the repeated month list) and the time (scoping both arms), generalized to ALL arm shapes (single/list/range/step/quartz) and wildcard months, with 'cualquier <weekday>' for a single weekday. Earlier fixes (the missing article, 'y también' for range+isolated hours, seconds nested into a fixed time, the anchored cadence) also shipped. Remaining residuals below.
note: BETA — model-validated by a blind 3-persona Sonnet panel, round-trip-clean over the expanded core set. Known residuals, not yet resolved: (1) the repeated 'a las' in a mixed-article clock list is the cost of rendering 'a la 01:00' correctly — eliding breaks the article; (2) a minute window confined to specific hours ('cada minuto del 0 al 30, a las …') reads restrictive-vs-additive — the 'solo' marker was tried and the blind re-review found it misread, so it was reverted; (3) a minute window crossed with an hour step ('cada minuto del 0 al 30, cada dos horas') has no clean idiom; (4) deeply stacked 4-level descriptions (seconds + minute + hour-list + day) and the wordy hour-step phrasing ('durante las horas de las 9, 11, 13…') remain verbose; (5) the minute-range register ('del 0 al 29') is inherent to expressing cron precisely. The OR-union defect (formerly the largest) is now fixed across all arm shapes. Graduates to stable only on fluent-human review.
